BUT the render loop line says if(speed<=0)return; — this means the canvas doesn't redraw when paused! The player can still use WASD (keyboard handler calls processTile and reveal directly), but they won't see anything until speed > 0.

So the issue is that keyboard input triggers game logic directly without calling the draw function, and if speed is 0, the render loop exits before drawing anything. I need to verify that closeWorldMap actually sets speed to 1 before enterTown is called, otherwise the town will be invisible.

Looking at the code flow, closeWorldMap does set speed to 1, which is good. But there's still a timing problem: with speed=1, the render loop waits 600ms between ticks, so WASD movement won't appear on screen immediately—the player has to wait up to 600ms to see their character move, which feels sluggish.

The real fix is to ensure the render loop keeps running even when speed=0, just skipping the game tick logic itself, so keyboard input triggers a visual update right away.

Here's everything that was fixed and rebuilt:

Critical Bug Fixes (5):

Currency mismatch — unified all currency['Bit'] → currency.bit. Economy now works — loot gold is spendable in shops.
Heal/mana potion values — useItem now reads item.val first (20HP potion actually heals 20, not 2d4).
Buff potion effect — changed effect:'buff_str' → effect:'buff', stat:'str'. Potions now apply buffs.
Quest progress tracking — handleEnemyDeath calls updateQuestProgress('kill',...) and addToInventory calls updateQuestProgress('loot',...). Quests can now complete.
Monster Den routing — enterTown now accepts forceType parameter. World map passes 'monster' explicitly so it doesn't get overridden by class detection.
Render Fix:

Game loop now renders EVERY frame regardless of speed. Previously speed=0 meant the canvas went black. WASD movement is now immediately visible.
UI Redesign — 3-Column Layout:

Left panel: Brain viz, HP/MP/NRG/XP bars, attributes, neurochemistry bars, memory log — always visible system state
Center: Game canvas (unchanged)
Right panel: Modular popup system with toolbar buttons (🧠Urg, 🎒Inv, ⚔Eqp, ✦Mag, 📜Qst, 👥Rep, ⚡Skl). Click to toggle panels on/off. Each panel is collapsible and closeable. Open what you want, scroll through them.
Bottom: Guide buttons on top, scrolling log below
Control bar: Speed/save/export/map/screenshot at very bottom with status indicator
World Map Fix:

Event listener deduplication (no more stacking handlers on repeated opens)
Named handler removal before re-adding
Comp2comp game v05

Wait, looking at the order again: spawnDungeonNPCs and dungeon.reveal happen before enterTown is called, so the robot should still be in the dungeon at that point. Then enterTown moves it to the town and sets the inTown flag, which means getCurrentMap() will return the town object for subsequent game loop iterations. Read canvas click handler

Script
Wait, I just realized something. The canvas getBoundingClientRect depends on the canvas's actual rendered size. The canvas has width=640 height=480 attributes, but it's inside a flex container #center-area which might stretch or shrink it. Let me check the CSS again:

css
#center-area{grid-column:2;grid-row:2;position:relative;display:flex;justify-content:center;align-items:center;background:#050508;overflow:hidden}
canvas#dungeon{image-rendering:pixelated;cursor:crosshair}
The canvas has no CSS width/height, so it will use its natural 640x480 size.

The center area might clip the canvas if it's smaller than 640x480, but the click coordinates should still map correctly since getBoundingClientRect returns the visible portion. Depending on the screen size and the 170px left sidebar plus 220px right sidebar, I've got around 1530px on a 1920px screen or 976px on a 1366px screen—plenty of room for the 640px canvas. I should just move forward with building this out.

Let me provide a clear summary of what was wrong and what was fixed.

Done
Here's what was actually broken and what I fixed:

Root cause of "cannot move": Player spawned at (16, 20) which was inside Rosa's Home building wall (the old layout had buildings covering that area). Every WASD press tried to move into adjacent wall tiles → rejected. Fixed by redesigning the town to a 3x3 grid of smaller buildings (6×4 each) with clear corridors, and spawning at y=19 in guaranteed open floor.

Root cause of "cannot do anything": The UI's updateUI() called engine.getAttack() and engine.getDefense() — methods that don't exist (actual names are getAttackBonus() and getAC()). This threw a TypeError every animation frame, silently crashing the entire render loop. Fixed the method names.

Root cause of "blank world map": The canvas fillStyle was set to 'var(--cyan)' — CSS variables don't work in Canvas 2D context. The player marker was invisible. Fixed to '#40d0e0'.

Additional fixes in this build:

Error handler wraps gameLoop so crashes log to the game console instead of silently dying
M key now opens world map from any location (not just town)
Guide buttons now map to actual urge names (explore→Wander, fight→Aggression, etc.)
Town spawn at y=19 gives 3 tiles of walkway before the south exit portal
Voice/music modules are intact and present (click 🔊 to initialize audio)
